original_text: You create a painting that wins a local art show.
identified_property: prestige and competitiveness of the art show

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: You create a painting that wins an informal art contest among coworkers.

variations.label: low
variations.text: You create a painting that wins a neighborhood art show.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: You create a painting that wins a small community art show.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: You create a painting that wins a local art show.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: You create a painting that wins a competitive statewide art competition.

variations.label: high
variations.text: You create a painting that wins a prestigious national art competition.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: You create a painting that wins a top prize at a world-renowned international art exhibition.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases the prestige and competitiveness of the art show from a casual office contest (tier 1) to the Venice Biennale (tier 7). The core scenario—creating a painting that wins an art show—remains constant throughout. Each step represents a meaningful increase in the scale, selectivity, and recognition associated with the competition.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because winning a globally renowned, highly competitive international art exhibition like the Venice Biennale confers far greater recognition, career impact, and artistic validation than winning smaller, less competitive shows.
v1_broken_audit_rationale: The ladder aims to vary prestige of artistic achievement, but T3 and T4 are out of order: winning a county-wide juried art show is plausibly more prestigious than winning a generic local art show. That breaks the required monotonic progression around the intended neutral midpoint.
v2_fix_explanation: The original T3 (county-wide juried) was arguably more prestigious than T4 (local), breaking monotonicity. I reset T4 to the fully unqualified 'an art show' (no prestige qualifier), then built T1-T3 as progressively smaller/more-constrained scopes below it (coworkers → small community → local) and T5-T7 as progressively larger/more-prestigious scopes above it (statewide → national → international), ensuring strict monotonic ordering.
